PermitPulse / Beta
California hub Guides Partners Sample report
Public launch California

PermitPulse is live in California with a tighter permit-history workflow.

The launch centers on one flagship offer: Permit History + Risk Report. The goal is simple. Give owners, buyers, architects, expediters, contractors, and diligence teams a clearer way to route a property, review permit history, and package the important risk signals without living inside raw portal screens.

Request Permit History + Risk Report Browse California jurisdictions
What PermitPulse does

It turns scattered permit records into a cleaner decision input.

  • Routes users to the right city or county lane faster.
  • Provides public California jurisdiction pages with official portal links and coverage notes.
  • Packages one property at a time into a Permit History + Risk Report when the decision matters.
Who it is for

Built for practical permit-intelligence use cases.

Owner-builders, acquisition teams, architects, permit expediters, ADU consultants, investor-friendly agents, and contractors all run into the same problem: the portal has pieces, but the decision needs a readable summary.

Current coverage

California launch pages are already live.

Core launch jurisdictions

Los Angeles, Los Angeles County, Sacramento, Santa Monica, Culver City, Beverly Hills, Pasadena, Glendale, Long Beach, and San Diego.

Open California hub

Practical guides

New launch guides cover permit history checks, city-versus-county routing, Sacramento portal use, Santa Monica due diligence, and California ADU research.

Open guides

Flagship offer

The Permit History + Risk Report is the shortest path when one address already matters and the portal alone is not enough.

See sample report
Where to start

Choose the shortest useful path.

  • Use /california/jurisdictions/ if you need the right local landing page first.
  • Use /guides/ if you need context before you decide what to order.
  • Use the Permit History + Risk Report if the property is already active in a quote, acquisition, owner review, or design handoff.
CTA

Ready to test the workflow on a real property?
